title: Capability Frontmatter (v1)
description: Validates the YAML frontmatter of a capability (the named middle term between a need and its fulfilling patterns) on every PR. Advisory: checks well-formedness, not the approval gate (a human PR review promotes a capability); see DESIGN.md §7. The proven-vs-candidate rule lives on the confidence field + the allOf block.

capability_key.description: Stable, cite-able key that survives renames. Cited by every requirement that fulfils this capability and by every pattern that back-references it. UPPER-KEBAB after the CAP- prefix, e.g. CAP-OLAP.

name.description: Human-readable capability name, e.g. 'Online Analytical Processing (OLAP)'.

capability_domain.description: Top-level family the capability belongs to. Sourced from capabilities/_schema/capability-domains.enum.txt.

summary.description: A short paragraph: what the capability is, at a glance. The body carries the full need, fulfilment options, and governance floor.

need_statement.description: The technology-free need in 'a system needs to ... so that ...' form. Names the problem, never the product. This is what makes the capability the stable middle term: the need outlives the component that fulfils it.

aliases.description: Lay synonyms a non-expert would type for this need, e.g. 'data warehouse', 'EDW', 'reporting store'. At least 2 are required: this list feeds capabilities/INDEX.md, the alias-searchable lookup a need-first reader is pointed at first. A capability with one alias is hard to find.

fulfilled_by.description: The components that fulfil this need, each with a confidence (proven or candidate; see the confidence field). minItems 1.

pattern_key.description: The fulfilling pattern's cite-able key. REQUIRED when confidence is 'proven'; OPTIONAL when 'candidate' (a candidate may name a vendor in `note` before any PAT- file exists). Must match an existing pattern's pattern_key.

confidence.description: 'proven' = a built, evidenced pattern fulfils this need today. 'candidate' = a plausible fulfilment that still owes a spike; it is not recommendable until proven. HUMAN-ONLY to set 'proven'; the agent writes 'candidate' and never flips it.

note.description: One line on the fulfilment. For a proven entry: why this pattern fulfils the need cleanly. For a candidate: the vendor component being considered and the headline caveat.

evidence.description: Artefacts proving the fulfilment is real. REQUIRED (at least one) when confidence is 'proven' (see allOf); omit for a candidate.

open_questions.description: The spike questions a candidate must answer before promotion to proven. Expected on a candidate; omitted on a proven entry.

governance_nfrs.description: The minimum governance bar any fulfilling pattern must meet or honestly waive — the floor a candidate is measured against at promotion. Each carries a kind from the same closed 11-kind NFR vocabulary the patterns use, a statement, and a testable acceptance_criterion.

kind.description: The NFR family — one of the closed 11 kinds shared with the pattern schema (patterns/_schema/nfr-kinds.enum.txt).

statement.description: What the governance bar asserts, e.g. 'PII columns carry policies enforced at query time'.

acceptance_criterion.description: The testable, measurable 'done', e.g. 'region == declared classification region'.

measurable_target.description: Optional concrete target, e.g. 'region == declared residency boundary'.

valid_from.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the capability was first authored.

validity_check_months.description: How often (in months) the capability should be re-checked for currency — its candidates may have matured, its need may have shifted. Defaults to 12 if omitted.

approval_status.description: Lifecycle state, set only by a human in PR review (never an agent). Per-value meanings: capabilities/README.

approved_by.description: Who blessed the capability in PR review (name or handle). REQUIRED when approval_status is provisional or approved. A human-owned fact.

approved_at.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the human approval. REQUIRED when approval_status is provisional or approved.

superseded_by.description: The capability_key that replaces this one. REQUIRED when approval_status = deprecated (see allOf).

allOf.$comment: approval_status in {provisional, approved} requires the human approval trail.
A deprecated capability must name its successor so a reader is never dead-ended.
Every proven fulfilment must name a pattern_key and carry build evidence — mirrors the pattern schema's evidence-required-once-promoted rule. A candidate needs neither.
